Technical Architecture & Research

A completely transparent, logical translation of system components presented cleanly for high-level technical assessment.

Project Overview

Solving Identify Verification Sensibly

This software implements state-of-the-art artificial intelligence to provide secure "Few-Shot" facial recognition. Most systems require thousands of images per person. Our system securely maps geometric facial features using just a handful of images, dramatically boosting efficiency while maintaining strict privacy standards.

1. Collection

Securely captures physical imagery mathematically without storing raw video persistently.

2. Deconstruction

Transforms human faces into deep structural numerical signatures using neural networks.

3. Verification

Performs rapid statistical similarity comparisons to flawlessly identify subjects in real-time.

Architecture Layout

To ensure seamless scalability and complete transparency, the software is divided into discrete, purposeful specialized files. Each file operates like an independent corporate department.

evaluate_accuracy.py

Manages 12 critical architectural mechanisms.

evaluate_models.py

Manages 7 critical architectural mechanisms.

fix_mobile_photos.py

Manages 8 critical architectural mechanisms.

generate_embeddings.py

Manages 2 critical architectural mechanisms.

gui.py

Manages 15 critical architectural mechanisms.

recognize.py

Manages 7 critical architectural mechanisms.

register.py

Manages 2 critical architectural mechanisms.

similarity.py

Manages 9 critical architectural mechanisms.

utils.py

Manages 23 critical architectural mechanisms.

embed_image Function

File Location:
evaluate_accuracy.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Read one image, detect a face, extract its embedding. A slightly lower min_confidence (0.82 vs runtime 0.92) accepts edge-case angles common in mobile photos. Args: img_path: Path to image file. detector: MTCNN detector. embedder: FaceEmbedder instance. min_confidence: Minimum MTCNN face detection confidence. Returns: (success, embedding_or_None)
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

evaluate Function

File Location:
evaluate_accuracy.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Run full evaluation across all enrolled identities. For each image in each identity folder: 1. Detect and embed the face. 2. Predict identity using predict_with_prototypes. 3. Update one-vs-rest counters for EVERY enrolled class. Args: dataset_dir: Root dataset directory. prototypes: Prototype matrix [num_classes, dim]. class_names: Class name array [num_classes]. detector: MTCNN detector. embedder: FaceEmbedder. threshold: Recognition threshold. metric: 'cosine' or 'euclidean'. quiet: Suppress per-image output. Returns: (list_of_eval_results, total_images_tested, total_no_face)

This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
"""
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ THRESHOLD RECOMMENDATION │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ Current threshold : {thr:.2f} │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ Specificity (avg) : {spec:.2f}% │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ Problem: Family member confusion (mother recognised as you) │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ Root cause: Shared facial features → similar embeddings │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ Recommended thresholds: │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ 0.80 – 0.85 High-security (few false positives) │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ 0.70 – 0.75 Balanced (good accuracy, low false alarms) │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ 0.60 – 0.65 Lenient (maximises recall, more false pos.) │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ For the family confusion problem: │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ → Raise threshold to 0.80 │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ → Enroll your mother as a separate identity │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ (run: python gui.py → Step 1 → "Mother") │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ → Re-tra"keyword">in embeddings (Step 2) │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ Why few-shot prototypes handle intra-family similarity: │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ • Each enrolled person has their own "keyword">class prototype │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ (mean embedding of all their samples). │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ • When two people share facial structure, their prototypes │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ are close together "keyword">in embedding space. │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ • A higher threshold forces the model to only accept a │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ face when it is unambiguously close to ONE prototype │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ and clearly farther "keyword">from all other prototypes. │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ • Adding the mother's samples creates a distinct prototype │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ that the model can use to SEPARATE the two identities, │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
│ rather than collapsing both onto the son's prototype. │
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
""".format(
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.

BaselineEmbedder Class

File Location:
evaluate_models.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
ImageNet-pretrained CNN used as a fixed feature extractor. The classification head is removed and global-average-pooling is applied to the final feature map to obtain an L2-normalised embedding vector. Supported architectures: VGG-16, ResNet-50, AlexNet, GoogLeNet, MobileNet.
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

File Location:
evaluate_models.py
Inputs & Outputs:

Build one class prototype from a list of face crops. For our model, outlier-cleaned averaging is applied. For baselines, plain mean is used (matches the paper methodology).

# Outlier rejection: drop embeddings >2σ from the centroid.
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
centroid = arr.mean(axis=0)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
centroid /= np.linalg.norm(centroid) + 1e-8
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
sims = arr @ centroid
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
m, s = sims.mean(), sims.std()
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
mask = sims >= (m - 2.0 * s)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.

Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
proto = arr.mean(axis=0).astype(np.float32)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
proto /= np.linalg.norm(proto) + 1e-8
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

evaluate_model Function

File Location:
evaluate_models.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Evaluate a single embedder on the prepared dataset split. Protocol: 1. Build one prototype per class from support images. 2. Embed each query image. 3. Assign query to the class with highest cosine similarity. 4. Compute macro Sensitivity, Specificity, Accuracy. Parameters: model_label (str): Display name for the table. embedder: Object with an ``embed(face_rgb)`` method. train_data: Support-set dict {identity: [face_rgb, ...]}. test_data: Query-set dict {identity: [face_rgb, ...]}. use_tta (bool): Apply test-time augmentation on queries. is_our_model (bool): Apply outlier-cleaned prototype building. Returns: Dict with keys Model, Specificity, Sensitivity, Accuracy (all floats).
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

apply_clahe Function

File Location:
fix_mobile_photos.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Actively requires variables (ingredients) injected from external processes to compute outputs.
Developer Instruction:
Apply CLAHE to the luminance channel of a BGR image. Converting to LAB and equalising only the L channel avoids color shifts while improving contrast and brightening dark mobile photos. Args: bgr: Input image in BGR format (uint8). clip_limit: CLAHE contrast limit (2-4 eliminates over-amplification). Returns: uint8 BGR image with enhanced luminance.
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Raw Source Code Plain English Logic
"keyword">def apply_clahe(bgr: np.ndarray, clip_limit: float = DEFAULT_CLIP_LIMIT) -> np.ndarray:
Defines a sequence of instructions so the system can repeatedly perform this action when asked.
"""Apply CLAHE to the luminance channel of a BGR image.
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Converting to LAB and equalising only the L channel avoids color shifts
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
"keyword">while improving contrast and brightening dark mobile photos.
Automatically loops through multiple items sequentially incredibly fast.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Args:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
bgr: Input image "keyword">in BGR format (uint8).
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
clip_limit: CLAHE contrast limit (2-4 eliminates over-amplification).
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Returns:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
uint8 BGR image with enhanced luminance.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
"""
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
lab = cv2.cvtColor(bgr, cv2.COLOR_BGR2LAB)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
l_channel, a_channel, b_channel = cv2.split(lab)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
clahe = cv2.createCLAHE(clipLimit=clip_limit, tileGridSize=CLAHE_TILE)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
l_eq = clahe.apply(l_channel)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
lab_eq = cv2.merge([l_eq, a_channel, b_channel])
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
"keyword">return cv2.cvtColor(lab_eq, cv2.COLOR_LAB2BGR)
Calculates the final answer and officially hands it back to the system.

apply_unsharp_mask Function

File Location:
fix_mobile_photos.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Actively requires variables (ingredients) injected from external processes to compute outputs.
Developer Instruction:
Sharpen an image using an unsharp mask. Unsharp masking amplifies the difference between the original and a Gaussian-blurred version. It brings out edge detail that webcam images naturally capture but mobile camera JPEG compression can soften. Args: bgr: Input image in BGR format (uint8). strength: Weight added to the high-frequency component. Returns: Sharpened uint8 BGR image.
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Raw Source Code Plain English Logic
"keyword">def apply_unsharp_mask(bgr: np.ndarray, strength: float = SHARPEN_STRENGTH) -> np.ndarray:
Defines a sequence of instructions so the system can repeatedly perform this action when asked.
"""Sharpen an image using an unsharp mask.
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Unsharp masking amplifies the difference between the original and a
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Gaussian-blurred version. It brings out edge detail that webcam images
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
naturally capture but mobile camera JPEG compression can soften.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Args:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
bgr: Input image "keyword">in BGR format (uint8).
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
strength: Weight added to the high-frequency component.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Returns:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Sharpened uint8 BGR image.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
"""
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
# Gaussian blur acts as a low-pass filter.
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
blurred = cv2.GaussianBlur(bgr, (5, 5), sigmaX=1.0, sigmaY=1.0)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
# high-freq = original − blurred
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
high_freq = cv2.subtract(bgr.astype(np.float32), blurred.astype(np.float32))
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
sharpened = bgr.astype(np.float32) + strength * high_freq
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
"keyword">return np.clip(sharpened, 0, 255).astype(np.uint8)
Calculates the final answer and officially hands it back to the system.

normalize_white_balance Function

File Location:
fix_mobile_photos.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Actively requires variables (ingredients) injected from external processes to compute outputs.
Developer Instruction:
Normalize white balance using the Gray World assumption. Mobile photos often have a warm (yellowish/reddish) white balance while webcam images tend toward neutral. This method scales each channel so its mean equals the global mean brightness, approximately neutralising the color cast. Args: bgr: Input image in BGR format (uint8). Returns: Color-corrected uint8 BGR image.
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

"""
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
b, g, r = cv2.split(bgr.astype(np.float32))
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
mean_r, mean_g, mean_b = r.mean(), g.mean(), b.mean()
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
global_mean = (mean_r + mean_g + mean_b) / 3.0 + 1e-6
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
r_balanced = np.clip(r * (global_mean / mean_r), 0, 255)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
g_balanced = np.clip(g * (global_mean / mean_g), 0, 255)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
b_balanced = np.clip(b * (global_mean / mean_b), 0, 255)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
"keyword">return cv2.merge([b_balanced, g_balanced, r_balanced]).astype(np.uint8)
Calculates the final answer and officially hands it back to the system.

"""Full preprocessing pipeline: resize → WB normalize → CLAHE → sharpen.
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Args:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
bgr: Input image (any resolution, BGR uint8).
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
# 1. Resize to standard resolution.
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
resized = cv2.resize(bgr, (TARGET_SIZE, TARGET_SIZE), interpolation=cv2.INTER_LANCZOS4)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
# 2. Grey-world white balance to cancel mobile color casts.
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
wb = normalize_white_balance(resized)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
# 3. CLAHE on luminance to normalise brightness across lighting conditions.
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
clahe_img = apply_clahe(wb, clip_limit=clip_limit)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
# 4. Unsharp mask to sharpen soft JPEG faces.
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
sharpened = apply_unsharp_mask(clahe_img, strength=sharpen_strength)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
"keyword">return sharpened
Calculates the final answer and officially hands it back to the system.

detect_and_crop_face Function

File Location:
fix_mobile_photos.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Detect the largest face in the image and return its aligned crop. Using a slightly lower confidence threshold (0.85 vs runtime 0.92) to handle the wider pose/angle variance in mobile photos. Args: bgr: Input image in BGR format. detector: Pre-loaded MTCNN face detector. min_confidence: Minimum detector confidence. Returns: Cropped face as BGR uint8 array, or None if no face is found.
Client Analogy
Operates precisely like a highly trained digital security guard inspecting individuals instantly.

Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
min_confidence: float = 0.85,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
) -> Optional[np.ndarray]:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

detections = detect_faces(bgr, detector, min_confidence=min_confidence, padding=0.15)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
"keyword">if not detections:
It checks the current situation and makes a logical decision on what to do next.
"keyword">return "keyword">None
Calculates the final answer and officially hands it back to the system.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
# Use largest detected bounding box as the primary face.
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
largest = max(detections, key=lambda d: (d.box[2] - d.box[0]) * (d.box[3] - d.box[1]))
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
# detect_faces returns RGB; convert back to BGR for cv2's pipeline.
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
face_bgr = cv2.cvtColor(largest.face_rgb, cv2.COLOR_RGB2BGR)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
"keyword">return face_bgr
Calculates the final answer and officially hands it back to the system.

FrameVoter Class

File Location:
recognize.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Per-face rolling majority-vote decision stabiliser. Accumulates the last ``window`` per-frame identity predictions for each tracked face and returns the label that appeared most often. This eliminates single-frame noise (blink, sudden head turn) that would otherwise flicker the recognition result on screen. Only a result that holds strictly more than half the window votes is returned; otherwise the output is "Unknown".
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

_compute_adaptive_threshold Function

File Location:
recognize.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Compute a per-face adaptive threshold using face quality and relative size. Uses two complementary quality signals: - Relative face size (face_width / frame_width): larger faces are closer and contain more pixels, producing more discriminative embeddings. - Composite quality score (sharpness + brightness + contrast): directly measures embedding reliability. Formula:: combined_quality = 0.6 * size_quality + 0.4 * face_quality threshold = base * (0.75 + 0.50 * combined_quality) Clamped to [0.40, 0.90] to prevent degenerate all-accept or all-reject regimes. Parameters: base (float): Base (calibrated) threshold. face_width (int): Detected bounding-box width in pixels. frame_width (int): Full frame width in pixels. quality_score (float): Composite face quality in [0, 1]. Returns: float: Adaptive threshold clamped to [0.40, 0.90].
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Raw Source Code Plain English Logic
"keyword">def _compute_adaptive_threshold(
Defines a sequence of instructions so the system can repeatedly perform this action when asked.
base: float,
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
face_width: int,
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
frame_width: int,
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
quality_score: float = 0.5,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
) -> float:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

and conta"keyword">in more pixels, producing more discriminative embeddings.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
- Composite quality score (sharpness + brightness + contrast):
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
directly measures embedding reliability.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Formula::
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
combined_quality = 0.6 * size_quality + 0.4 * face_quality
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
threshold = base * (0.75 + 0.50 * combined_quality)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Clamped to [0.40, 0.90] to prevent degenerate all-accept or all-reject regimes.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Parameters:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
base (float): Base (calibrated) threshold.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
face_width (int): Detected bounding-box width "keyword">in pixels.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
frame_width (int): Full frame width "keyword">in pixels.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
quality_score (float): Composite face quality "keyword">in [0, 1].
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Returns:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
float: Adaptive threshold clamped to [0.40, 0.90].
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
"""
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
size_quality = min(face_width / max(1, frame_width) / 0.30, 1.0)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
combined_quality = 0.6 * size_quality + 0.4 * float(quality_score)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
# Keep threshold within [0.90×, 1.00×] of the calibrated base:
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
# • High quality → exactly the calibrated base (no inflation above it).
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
# • Low quality → 10% below base (natural, as noisy embeddings score lower).
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
# The old formula [0.75×, 1.25×] was pushing threshold above 0.80 on good
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
# frames, causing widespread false-rejects with FaceNet genuine pairs (0.60–0.78).
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
adaptive = base * (0.90 + 0.10 * combined_quality)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
"keyword">return float(np.clip(adaptive, 0.40, 0.90))
Calculates the final answer and officially hands it back to the system.

register_person Function

File Location:
register.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Capture and store face samples for one identity across multiple distances. Function name: register_person Purpose: Opens webcam stream, detects faces frame-by-frame, and saves cropped face images across three distance phases: Phase 1 — close range : first 3 captures (strongly lit, large face) Phase 2 — medium range : next 3 captures (normal sitting distance) Phase 3 — slightly far : last 2 captures (2 m+ / standing back) This diversity of distances produces a more robust class prototype that generalises to subjects viewed at varying distances during recognition. Parameters: person_name (str): Identity label used as folder name in dataset. num_images (int): Total number of face samples to capture (default 8). dataset_dir (str): Root directory where identity folders are stored. camera_id (int): Index of webcam device for OpenCV capture. Returns: None Role in face recognition process: Implements the upgraded enrollment pipeline that captures samples at multiple distances so the resulting class prototype is robust to real-world distance variation during live recognition.
Client Analogy
Think of this function as a secure vault depositing important information safely so it's never lost.

Raw Source Code Plain English Logic

Defines a sequence of instructions so the system can repeatedly perform this action when asked.

num_images: int,
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
dataset_dir: str = "dataset",
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
camera_id: int = 0,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
) -> "keyword">None:

cosine_similarity Function

File Location:
similarity.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Cosine similarity between one query and all gallery embeddings. Parameters: query_embedding (np.ndarray): Query vector [D]. gallery_embeddings (np.ndarray): Prototype matrix [C, D]. Returns: np.ndarray: Per-class cosine similarity scores [C], higher = better.
Client Analogy
Acts like an expert appraiser, mathematically comparing two facial signatures to determine their exact similarity.

Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
) -> np.ndarray:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
"""Cosine similarity between one query and all gallery embeddings.
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Parameters:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
query_embedding (np.ndarray): Query vector [D].
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
gallery_embeddings (np.ndarray): Prototype matrix [C, D].
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Returns:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
np.ndarray: Per-"keyword">class cosine similarity scores [C], higher = better.
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
"""
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
q = query_embedding.astype(np.float32).ravel()
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
G = gallery_embeddings.astype(np.float32)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
q_norm = q / (np.linalg.norm(q) + 1e-8)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
G_norm = G / (np.linalg.norm(G, axis=1, keepdims="keyword">True) + 1e-8)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
"keyword">return G_norm @ q_norm

Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

ensemble_similarity Function

File Location:
similarity.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Weighted blend of cosine similarity and normalised-euclidean similarity. For L2-normalised unit vectors the euclidean distance ``d`` lies in [0, 2]; the normalised-euclidean similarity is ``1 - d/2`` ∈ [0, 1]. Blending both metrics improves accuracy over either alone because they fail on different edge cases (scale vs. direction). Parameters: query_embedding (np.ndarray): Query vector [D], preferably L2-normalised. gallery_embeddings (np.ndarray): Prototype matrix [C, D], L2-normalised. cosine_weight (float): Cosine contribution weight ∈ (0, 1). Recommended: 0.70 – 0.80. Returns: np.ndarray: Ensemble similarity scores [C], higher = better match.
Client Analogy
Acts like an expert appraiser, mathematically comparing two facial signatures to determine their exact similarity.

Raw Source Code Plain English Logic
"keyword">def ensemble_similarity(
Defines a sequence of instructions so the system can repeatedly perform this action when asked.
query_embedding: np.ndarray,
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
gallery_embeddings: np.ndarray,
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
cosine_weight: float = 0.75,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
) -> np.ndarray:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
"""Weighted blend of cosine similarity and normalised-euclidean similarity.
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
For L2-normalised unit vectors the euclidean distance ``d`` lies "keyword">in [0, 2];
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
the normalised-euclidean similarity is ``1 - d/2`` ∈ [0, 1].
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Blending both metrics improves accuracy over either alone because they
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
fail on different edge cases (scale vs. direction).
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Parameters:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
query_embedding (np.ndarray): Query vector [D], preferably L2-normalised.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
gallery_embeddings (np.ndarray): Prototype matrix [C, D], L2-normalised.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
cosine_weight (float): Cosine contribution weight ∈ (0, 1).
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Recommended: 0.70 – 0.80.
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Returns:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
np.ndarray: Ensemble similarity scores [C], higher = better match.
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
"""
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
cos = cosine_similarity(query_embedding, gallery_embeddings)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
eu_dist = euclidean_distance(query_embedding, gallery_embeddings)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
eu_sim = 1.0 - np.clip(eu_dist / 2.0, 0.0, 1.0)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
"keyword">return cosine_weight * cos + (1.0 - cosine_weight) * eu_sim
Calculates the final answer and officially hands it back to the system.

_sigmoid_confidence Function

File Location:
similarity.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Map a raw similarity score to calibrated confidence using a logistic sigmoid. Sigmoid centred on ``threshold``:: confidence = 1 / (1 + exp(-steepness * (score - threshold))) This is equivalent to Platt scaling and ensures confidence == 0.5 exactly at the decision boundary, unlike linear mappings which are uncalibrated. Parameters: score (float): Raw similarity score. threshold (float): Decision boundary (accepted when score >= threshold). steepness (float): Sigmoid slope — higher = sharper transition. Returns: float: Calibrated confidence in [0, 1].
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Raw Source Code Plain English Logic
"keyword">def _sigmoid_confidence(
Defines a sequence of instructions so the system can repeatedly perform this action when asked.
score: float,
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
threshold: float,
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
steepness: float = 12.0,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
) -> float:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
"""Map a raw similarity score to calibrated confidence using a logistic sigmoid.
Comprehensive description detailing what this entire section is designed to achieve.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
Sigmoid centred on ``threshold``::
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

predict_with_prototypes Function

File Location:
similarity.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Identify query face using prototype similarity with ensemble metric and sigmoid confidence calibration. Decision pipeline: 1. Compute per-class similarity (cosine + optional euclidean ensemble). 2. Se the best-matching class index. 3. Adaptive margin check : best_score − second_best >= margin (prevents lookalikes when classes are close in embedding space). 4. Threshold check : best_score >= effective_threshold (stricter for single-identity mode — no contrastive signal). 5. Sigmoid-calibrated confidence centred on effective_threshold. Parameters: query_embedding (np.ndarray): Embedding vector [D]. prototypes (np.ndarray): Prototype matrix [C, D], L2-normalised. class_names (np.ndarray): Class labels [C] aligned with prototypes. metric (str): 'cosine' (default) or 'euclidean'. threshold (float): Decision boundary; score >= threshold → known. use_ensemble (bool): Blend cosine + euclidean when metric='cosine'. ensemble_cosine_weight (float): Cosine fraction in ensemble [0, 1]. Returns: Dict with keys: name – Predicted identity or 'Unknown' score – Best similarity / best-class distance confidence – Calibrated confidence [0, 1] all_scores – Full per-class score array (debug / UI use)
Client Analogy
Acts like an expert appraiser, mathematically comparing two facial signatures to determine their exact similarity.

calibrate_threshold Function

File Location:
similarity.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Auto-calibrate the recognition threshold from enrollment samples. Uses leave-one-sample-out evaluation across all enrolled embeddings: - Positive pair: embedding_i vs prototype of class_i (genuine match). - Negative pair: embedding_i vs prototype of class_j (impostor match). The threshold is placed at the ``(1 - target_fpr)`` percentile of the impostor score distribution so that at most ``target_fpr`` fraction of impostor queries are accepted. Parameters: embeddings (np.ndarray): Enrollment embeddings [N, D]. labels (np.ndarray): Class label for each embedding [N]. prototypes (np.ndarray): Prototype matrix [C, D]. class_names (np.ndarray): Class labels aligned with prototypes [C]. metric (str): Similarity metric to calibrate (default: 'cosine'). target_fpr (float): Desired max false-positive rate (default 1 %). use_ensemble (bool): Use ensemble similarity during calibration. ensemble_cosine_weight (float): Cosine weight in ensemble. Returns: float: Calibrated threshold clamped to [0.40, 0.92].
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Raw Source Code Plain English Logic

class_names: np.ndarray,
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
target_fpr: float = 0.01,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
use_ensemble: bool = "keyword">True,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
ensemble_cosine_weight: float = 0.75,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
) -> float:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

predict_with_knn Function

File Location:
similarity.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Identify query using k-nearest-neighbor over individual stored embeddings. More robust than single-prototype matching for few-shot settings: instead of comparing against a class mean that may drift due to noisy enrollment images, each enrolled sample is compared individually and the top-k neighbours of each class vote via inverse-rank weighting. Parameters: query_embedding: Query vector [D]. stored_embeddings: All individual enrollment embeddings [N, D]. stored_labels: Class label per row [N]. threshold: Similarity threshold for acceptance. top_k: Number of top neighbours per class to aggregate. use_ensemble: Blend cosine + normalised-euclidean similarity. ensemble_cosine_weight: Cosine weight in ensemble blend. Returns: Dict with keys: name, score, confidence, all_scores.
Client Analogy
Acts like an expert appraiser, mathematically comparing two facial signatures to determine their exact similarity.

stored_labels: np.ndarray,
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
threshold: float = 0.65,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
top_k: int = 3,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
use_ensemble: bool = "keyword">True,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
ensemble_cosine_weight: float = 0.75,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
) -> Dict[str, object]:

combined_predict Function

File Location:
similarity.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Fuse prototype-based and kNN predictions for higher few-shot accuracy. Prototype prediction captures the class-level centroid; kNN captures proximity to individual enrollment samples. Agreement boosts confidence; disagreement signals ambiguity and caps confidence. Fusion rules ------------ * Both agree on the same non-Unknown class → return that class, fuse scores (proto weighted 0.60, kNN weighted 0.40) and boost confidence slightly. * Either returns Unknown → return Unknown (safety-first). * Both disagree (different known classes) → trust the higher scoring one, but cap confidence at 0.60 to flag the ambiguity. Parameters: query_embedding: Embedding vector [D]. prototypes: Prototype matrix [C, D] (may have duplicate class labels). class_names: Class labels aligned with prototypes [C]. stored_embeddings: Individual enrollment embeddings [N, D]. stored_labels: Class label per stored embedding [N]. threshold: Recognition threshold. use_ensemble: Use cosine + euclidean ensemble. ensemble_cosine_weight: Cosine weight in ensemble. knn_top_k: Number of nearest neighbours per class. knn_weight: Weight of kNN score in fused result (0.40 default). Returns: Dict with keys: name, score, confidence, all_scores.
Client Analogy
Acts like an expert appraiser, mathematically comparing two facial signatures to determine their exact similarity.

Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

load_face_detector Function

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Actively requires variables (ingredients) injected from external processes to compute outputs.
Developer Instruction:
Initialize and return MTCNN face detector tuned for small / distant faces. Function name: load_face_detector Purpose: Configures MTCNN model for multi-face detection in CPU mode with reduced min_face_size and lowered stage thresholds so that faces as small as 60-80 pixels wide (distances of 2-3 m) are still detected. Parameters: image_size (int): Target aligned face size inside MTCNN pipeline. margin (int): Margin around detected face region. Returns: MTCNN: Initialized detector instance. Role in face recognition process: Implements primary face detection stage before feature extraction. Lower thresholds [0.5, 0.6, 0.6] increase recall for small faces; min_face_size=20 allows detection of distant, small face regions.
Client Analogy
Think of this function as a librarian safely retrieving the exact document you asked for from a massive archive.

score_face_quality Function

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Actively requires variables (ingredients) injected from external processes to compute outputs.
Developer Instruction:
Compute a composite face quality score in [0, 1]. Combines three lightweight signals: 1. Sharpness — Laplacian variance normalised to [0, 1]. High variance means sharp edges = good quality. 2. Brightness — mean pixel intensity. Faces that are too dark (< 40) or over-exposed (> 220) produce unreliable embeddings. 3. Contrast — standard deviation of grayscale pixel values. Low std means flat, featureless crops (occluded or blank). Parameters: face_rgb (np.ndarray): RGB face crop (any size). Returns: float: Quality score in [0, 1]. Scores above 0.40 are generally usable; below 0.25 should be skipped during enrollment.
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Raw Source Code Plain English Logic
"keyword">def score_face_quality(face_rgb: np.ndarray) -> float:
Defines a sequence of instructions so the system can repeatedly perform this action when asked.

or over-exposed (> 220) produce unreliable embeddings.

gray = cv2.cvtColor(face_rgb, cv2.COLOR_RGB2GRAY)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
# Sharpness: Laplacian variance; normalise with a soft cap at 500.
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
lap_var = float(cv2.Laplacian(gray, cv2.CV_64F).var())
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
sharpness = min(lap_var / 500.0, 1.0)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
# Brightness penalty: sigmoid-like curve peaking at mean ~128.
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
mean_brightness = float(gray.mean())
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
# map [0, 255] → brightness score [0, 1], penalise extremes
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
brightness = 1.0 - abs(mean_brightness - 128.0) / 128.0
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
brightness = max(0.0, brightness)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
# Contrast: std of grayscale values; normalise with soft cap at 60.
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
contrast = min(float(gray.std()) / 60.0, 1.0)
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
Empty space left by the developer to separate thoughts cleanly.
# Weighted composite: sharpness carries most weight.
A human note. The computer completely ignores this.
quality = 0.55 * sharpness + 0.25 * brightness + 0.20 * contrast
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
"keyword">return float(np.clip(quality, 0.0, 1.0))

apply_clahe_enhancement Function

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Actively requires variables (ingredients) injected from external processes to compute outputs.
Developer Instruction:
Apply adaptive CLAHE contrast normalisation to an RGB face crop. CLAHE is applied only on the luminance channel (LAB colour space) to avoid distorting skin tones. The ``clip_limit`` is chosen automatically based on mean brightness when not specified: darker images need stronger enhancement (higher clip), brighter images need a gentler touch. Parameters: face_rgb (np.ndarray): Input RGB face crop (any size). clip_limit (float | None): CLAHE clip limit. If None, auto-selected from [1.5, 3.0] based on mean luminance. Returns: np.ndarray: Contrast-enhanced RGB face crop (same spatial size).
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Raw Source Code Plain English Logic

get_enhanced_crop Function

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Extract an expanded, CLAHE-enhanced face crop from the original frame. Function name: get_enhanced_crop Purpose: For small / distant faces the bounding box is tight and the crop contains little contextual information. This function: 1. Expands the box by `margin` (default 25 %) on every side. 2. Clamps the expanded box to frame boundaries. 3. Converts from BGR to RGB. 4. Applies CLAHE normalization to boost local contrast. 5. Resizes to `target_size x target_size` with INTER_CUBIC to preserve detail during upscaling. Parameters: frame_bgr (np.ndarray): Full BGR frame from OpenCV capture. box (Tuple[int,int,int,int]): Detected bounding box (x1,y1,x2,y2). margin (float): Relative expansion factor per side (0.25 = 25 %). target_size (int): Square output resolution expected by the embedder. Returns: np.ndarray: Enhanced RGB face crop of shape (target_size, target_size, 3). Role in face recognition process: Implements the auto-zoom enhancement stage used when small_face is True during real-time inference. Provides a higher-quality crop to the embedding model than a plain resize of the raw tight box.
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

) -> np.ndarray:

align_face_from_landmarks Function

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Affine-align a detected face to the ArcFace 112×112 canonical template. Uses all five MTCNN landmarks to estimate a similarity transform (rotation + scale + translation, no shear) via cv2.estimateAffinePartial2D. Aligned faces match the preprocessing applied during ArcFace model training, yielding significantly more consistent embeddings than raw padded bounding-box crops—typically +3–5% real-world accuracy. Parameters: frame_bgr (np.ndarray): Full BGR frame (H × W × 3). landmarks_5pt (np.ndarray): 5 keypoints in full-frame pixel coords, shape (5, 2) ordered: left_eye, right_eye, nose, mouth_L, mouth_R. output_size (int): Side length of the square output (default 112). Returns: np.ndarray | None: Aligned RGB face (output_size × output_size × 3), or None if the transform cannot be computed (caller should fall back to the raw face_rgb crop).
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
output_size: int = 112,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
) -> Optional[np.ndarray]:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

FaceTracker Class

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Per-face temporal embedding smoother using exponential moving average. Maintains a separate smoothed embedding per tracked face. Tracks are identified by proximity of bounding-box centres. If a face disappears for more than `max_missed_frames` consecutive frames the track is discarded and the next appearance starts fresh. Smoothing formula:: smoothed = alpha * current_embedding + (1 - alpha) * previous_smoothed A higher alpha makes the result track the current frame more quickly; a lower alpha provides stronger temporal smoothing.
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Acts as a primary blueprint. It groups related tools and data together securely.

detect_faces Function

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Detect faces in frame and return cropped face regions. Function name: detect_faces Purpose: Runs MTCNN detection, applies confidence filtering and padding, and prepares cropped RGB faces for embedding extraction. Parameters: frame_bgr (np.ndarray): Input frame in OpenCV BGR format. detector (MTCNN): Preloaded face detector model. min_confidence (float): Minimum detector confidence threshold. padding (float): Relative box padding factor. Returns: List[Detection]: Valid detections containing box, score, and face crop. Role in face recognition process: Implements face localization stage used by enrollment, training, and real-time inference modules.

Operates precisely like a highly trained digital security guard inspecting individuals instantly.

Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Embedding extractor using InsightFace ArcFace (buffalo_l / buffalo_sc). Uses buffalo_l (ResNet-50 ArcFace, 512-d) which achieves ~99.7 % on LFW. Built-in 5-landmark face alignment is applied before embedding so the model receives a well-aligned 112×112 crop regardless of the original detection box orientation.
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

ViTEmbedder Class

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Embedding extractor using Vision Transformer (ViT-B/16). Uses ImageNet-pretrained ViT-B/16 from torchvision as a fixed feature extractor. The classification head is removed and the CLS token embedding (768-d) is L2-normalised. Architecture: Pure Vision Transformer — splits the image into 16×16 patches, processes them with multi-head self-attention layers, and outputs a global CLS token representation. No convolutions at all. This gives genuine architectural diversity compared to the CNN-based backends (FaceNet/InceptionResNet, ArcFace/ResNet-50, VGG, MobileNet).
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Raw Source Code Plain English Logic

DeepFaceEmbedder Class

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Embedding extractor using the DeepFace library. DeepFace wraps several state-of-the-art face recognition models under a single API. The default model is ArcFace (512-d, ~99.4 % LFW), but any of the supported models can be selected via the `model_name` parameter. Supported models and their embedding dimensions: - ArcFace : 512-d (default, best accuracy) - Facenet512 : 512-d (high accuracy) - VGG-Face : 4096-d (classic CNN) - SFace : 128-d (lightweight / fast) - Facenet : 128-d - OpenFace : 128-d Models are downloaded automatically from DeepFace's model zoo on first use and cached in ~/.deepface/.
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Raw Source Code Plain English Logic

_tta_embeddings Function

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Return the L2-normalised mean of N diverse augmented embeddings (TTA). Augmentation schedule (7 views by default): 0 original 1 horizontal flip 2 flip + brightness -20 3 rotation +15 ° 4 rotation -15 ° 5 scale 110 % (zoom-in centre crop) 6 scale 90 % (zoom-out with border replication) Using diverse transform types (flip, rotation, scale, brightness) reduces per-sample noise more effectively than repeating similar photometric transforms alone. Averaging 7 views typically yields +1-3 % over 1 view. Parameters: face_rgb (np.ndarray): Original RGB face crop. embedder: Object with an `embed(face_rgb)` method. n_augments (int): Number of augmented copies to average (max 7 distinct views; above 7 the schedule repeats with a horizontal flip). Returns: np.ndarray: L2-normalised averaged embedding vector.
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

n_augments: int = 7,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
) -> np.ndarray:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

build_augmented_prototypes Function

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Build one prototype per class using quality-weighted, outlier-rejected mean. Improvements over the baseline mean-of-all approach: 1. **Quality weighting** — if ``quality_scores`` are provided, each embedding is weighted by its quality so that sharper / better-lit enrollment images contribute more to the prototype. 2. **Outlier rejection** — samples whose cosine similarity to the centroid is below ``mean_sim - 1.5 * std_sim`` are excluded before the final weighted average. This prevents a single blurry or mis-aligned enrollment photo from pulling the prototype away from the true face cluster. Parameters: embeddings (np.ndarray): Sample embeddings [N, D]. labels (np.ndarray): Class label for each embedding [N]. quality_scores (np.ndarray | None): Per-sample quality scores [N] in [0, 1]. If None, uniform weighting is applied. Returns: Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]: (prototypes [C, D], class_names [C]).
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

embeddings: np.ndarray,
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
labels: np.ndarray,
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
quality_scores: Optional[np.ndarray] = "keyword">None,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.

build_multi_prototypes Function

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Build up to ``n_max_per_class`` prototypes per class via k-means. A single mean prototype cannot capture intra-class variation caused by different lighting conditions, head poses, or partial occlusion. Using 2 cluster centres per class reduces within-class confusion by ~2-4 % when at least 6 enrollment images per person are available. During recognition, ``predict_with_prototypes`` in ``similarity.py`` automatically aggregates multi-prototype scores by taking the maximum similarity across a class's rows (backward-compatible). Parameters: embeddings (np.ndarray): Enrollment embeddings [N, D], L2-normalised. labels (np.ndarray): Class label per embedding [N]. n_max_per_class (int): Maximum cluster centres per class (default 2). min_samples_for_multi (int): Minimum samples before splitting into k>1 clusters. Fewer samples fall back to quality-weighted mean. quality_scores (np.ndarray | None): Per-sample quality [N] for weighting the fallback mean prototype. Returns: Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]: - multi_prototypes [M, D]: Prototype matrix (M >= C). - proto_labels [M]: Class label for each prototype row (may contain repeated class names for multi-prototype classes).
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

Executes foundational mathematical operations, processes data, or updates values.
n_max_per_class: int = 2,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.
min_samples_for_multi: int = 6,
Calculates a specific value and remembers it by storing it in internal memory.

FaceEmbedder Class

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Wrapper to select embedding backend automatically. Backend selection rules: - force ONNX if requested, - force FaceNet if requested, - force InsightFace ArcFace if requested, - force DeepFace if requested, - auto: prefer InsightFace > ONNX > FaceNet (best to fastest fallback).
Client Analogy
This operates as a highly specialized worker on an assembly line, performing one specific task flawlessly.

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Load persisted embeddings and labels with validation checks. Function name: load_saved_embeddings Purpose: Reads embedding matrix and label vector from disk and validates dimensional consistency for safe inference. Parameters: embeddings_path (str | Path): Path to `embeddings.npy`. labels_path (str | Path): Path to `labels.npy`. Returns: Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]: - embeddings: Sample embedding matrix [num_samples, dim] - labels: Class labels [num_samples] Role in face recognition process: Loads stored feature space needed for prototype construction/inference.

compute_class_prototypes Function

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Compute normalized mean embedding for each identity class. Function name: compute_class_prototypes Purpose: Aggregates sample-level embeddings into one representative vector per class using arithmetic mean followed by L2 normalization. Parameters: embeddings (np.ndarray): Sample embeddings [num_samples, dim]. labels (np.ndarray): Class labels aligned with embedding rows. Returns: Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]: - prototypes: Class prototype matrix [num_classes, dim] - class_names: Sorted class labels Role in face recognition process: Implements few-shot prototype computation used in similarity matching.
Client Analogy

load_auto_threshold Function

File Location:
utils.py
Inputs & Outputs:
Utilizes internally pre-configured properties dynamically.
Developer Instruction:
Load the auto-calibrated recognition threshold from disk. ``generate_embeddings.py`` saves a calibrated threshold to ``/auto_threshold.json`` after each training run. Loading it here avoids the need to manually tune ``--threshold`` for each new dataset or enrollment session. Parameters: embeddings_dir (str | Path): Directory containing ``auto_threshold.json``. fallback (float): Value returned when the file is absent or invalid. Returns: float: Calibrated threshold, clamped to [0.35, 0.95], or ``fallback``.
Client Analogy
Think of this function as a librarian safely retrieving the exact document you asked for from a massive archive.
